<!-- Code by Lara Ahmed and Thomas Brochhagen, adapted from Daniel Padfield https://padpadpadpad.github.io/BayesTraitsAcrAllNodes/ -->

<!-- Arabic tree sourced from lgfam-newick (Dediu) using Glottolog topology with Grafen (1989) branch lengths. lgfam-newick repo: https://github.com/ddediu/lgfam-newick -->

<!-- Assumes:
  1. python3 is installed
  2. BayesTraits binary exists at path specified by BAYESTRAITS_PATH
  3. Grambank metadata file exists at path specified by GRAMBANK_PATH
  4. lgfam-newick repo exists at path specified by LGFAMNEWICK_PATH
-->

## 1. Setup

# Install and load packages
install.packages("rmarkdown")
install.packages("here")
install.packages("tidyverse")
install.packages("ape")
install.packages("phytools")
install.packages("phangorn")
install.packages("GA")
install.packages("stringr")
install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("rgriff23/btw")

library(here)
library(tidyverse)
library(ape)
library(phytools)
library(phangorn)
library(GA)
library(stringr)
library(btw)

# Set where I am in the project
here::i_am('scripts/BayesTraits.Rmd')

# Set customization constants
BAYESTRAITS_PATH <- "../BayesTraitsV4.0.0-OSX/BayesTraitsV4"
GRAMBANK_PATH <- "../../datasets/grambank-grambank-7ae000c/cldf/StructureDataset-metadata.json"
LGFAMNEWICK_PATH <- "../../lgfam-newick"


## 2. Phylogenetic tree loading

# Load Afro-Asiatic tree from lgfam-newick (Grafen branch lengths, Glottolog topology)
lgfamnewick_code_path <- paste(
  LGFAMNEWICK_PATH,
  "/code/FamilyTrees.R",
  sep = ""
  )
source(lgfamnewick_code_path)

lgfamnewick_tree_path <- paste(
  LGFAMNEWICK_PATH, 
  "/output/glottolog/glottolog-newick-grafen.csv",
  sep = ""
  )
classif <- languageclassification(
  csv.file = lgfamnewick_tree_path,
  csv.tree.column = "Tree"
  )

afroasiatic <- classif[[which(sapply(classif, function(x) attr(x, "tree.name")) == "Afro-Asiatic")]]

# Clean labels: phytools keeps surrounding quotes; lgfam-newick uses `` for apostrophes
clean_labels <- function(x) {
  x <- gsub("^'|'$", "", x) # strip surrounding single quotes
  x <- gsub(" \\[.*", "", x) # strip glottolog metadata [i-...][w-...] etc.
  x <- gsub("``", "'", x, fixed=TRUE) # unescape backtick-pairs to apostrophe
  x <- gsub(" ", "_", x)
  x
}
afroasiatic$tip.label  <- clean_labels(afroasiatic$tip.label)
afroasiatic$node.label <- clean_labels(afroasiatic$node.label)

# Extract the Arabic sub-clade
tree <- extract.clade(afroasiatic, "Arabic")

# In the full Glottolog tree many Grambank language-level units (e.g. "Egyptian_Arabic",
# "Gulf_Arabic") are internal nodes, not leaves. This helper collapses each such clade
# to a single tip, preserving the edge length leading *to* that ancestral node.
collapse_clade_to_tip <- function(tree, lang_name) {
  if (lang_name %in% tree$tip.label) return(tree)
  nd <- which(tree$node.label == lang_name)
  if (!length(nd)) { warning(paste("Node not found:", lang_name)); return(tree) }
  nd_idx    <- nd + Ntip(tree)
  edge_len  <- tree$edge.length[tree$edge[, 2] == nd_idx]
  desc_tips <- extract.clade(tree, nd_idx)$tip.label
  if (length(desc_tips) > 1) tree <- drop.tip(tree, desc_tips[-1])
  tip_pos   <- which(tree$tip.label == desc_tips[1])
  tree$edge.length[which(tree$edge[, 2] == tip_pos)] <- edge_len
  tree$tip.label[tip_pos] <- lang_name
  tree
}

# Collapse language groups that are internal nodes in the Glottolog tree
# Changes recorded here: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1HY9ZQ_Vhrd_n2OKdKs_z37s2da0056ZRCeZTNWtMoaQ/edit?gid=0#gid=0
for (lang in c("Gulf_Arabic",
               "Eastern_Egyptian_Bedawi_Arabic",
               "Egyptian_Arabic",
               "Chadian_Arabic",
               "Levantine_Arabic",
               "Libyan_Arabic",
               "Maltese",
               "Standard_Arabic"
               )) {
  tree <- collapse_clade_to_tip(tree, lang)
}

# Keep only the 14 languages with Grambank codings
tree <- keep.tip(tree, c("Sanaani_Arabic", "Baharna_Arabic", "Gulf_Arabic",
                          "Eastern_Egyptian_Bedawi_Arabic", "Egyptian_Arabic",
                          "Chadian_Arabic", "Nubi", "South_Sudanese_Creole_Arabic",
                          "Cypriot_Arabic", "Levantine_Arabic",
                          "Libyan_Arabic", "Hassaniyya", "Maltese", "Standard_Arabic"))

# Rename "Levantine_Arabic" to "North_Levantine_Arabic" to match Grambank
tree$tip.label[tree$tip.label == "Levantine_Arabic"] <- "North_Levantine_Arabic"

# Cast to plain phylo (FamilyTrees.R returns a familytree subclass; downstream ape code expects phylo)
class(tree) <- "phylo"

# Recompute Grafen branch lengths on the pruned 14-tip tree
# (lgfam-newick values 1-140 are scaled for the full Glottolog tree; pruned-tree values are needed)
tree <- ape::compute.brlen(tree, method = "Grafen")

# Plot tree
tree_plot <- plot(tree, show.node.label = TRUE, root.edge = TRUE)


## 3. Trait data loading

# Run Python script that pulls Grambank features
system(paste("python3 extract_features_from_grambank.py", GRAMBANK_PATH))
features <- read.csv("../data/features.csv")

for (id in features$id) {
  # For each feature, run Python script to generate trait file
  system(paste("python3 generate_trait_file.py", GRAMBANK_PATH, id))
  
  # Read in trait file as table
  trait_file_name <- paste(paste("data/trait", id, sep = "_"), ".txt", sep = "")
  trait <- read.table(here(trait_file_name), col.names = c("language", "value"))
  
  # Check that the trait table contains exactly the same taxa as the tree (BayesTraits matches by name)
  if (!setequal(trait$language, tree$tip.label)) {
    stop("Trait does not have same taxa as tree")
  }
}


## 4. BayesTraits input formatting

# In addition to the feature-specific trait files generated above, BayesTraits expects a nexus tree file (which will be the same across all features), as well as feature-specific control files

# First, generate the feature-agnostic nexus tree file

# Save tree as nexus file as required by BayesTraits
# write.nexus() adds a BEGIN TAXA block and embeds node labels that BayesTraits 4.1.1 rejects;
# write the minimal TREES-only format manually instead.
tree$node.label <- NULL
newick_str <- write.tree(tree)
newick_num  <- newick_str
for (i in seq_along(tree$tip.label)) {
  newick_num <- gsub(paste0("\\b", tree$tip.label[i], "\\b"), as.character(i), newick_num)
}
translate_block <- paste0(seq_along(tree$tip.label), "\t", tree$tip.label, collapse = ",\n\t\t")
writeLines(paste0(
  "#NEXUS\n\nBEGIN TREES;\n\tTRANSLATE\n\t\t", translate_block,
  "\n\t;\n\tTREE * UNTITLED = [&U] ", newick_num, "\nEND;\n"
), here("data/tree.nex"))

# Then, generate the feature-agnostic part of the control file

# For each internal node, get its descendant tip labels (uses phangorn, no ggtree needed)
internal_nodes <- seq(Ntip(tree) + 1, Ntip(tree) + Nnode(tree))
d_nodes <- tibble(
  nodes = internal_nodes,
  label = sapply(internal_nodes, function(n) {
    tip_idx <- phangorn::Descendants(tree, n, type = "tips")[[1]]
    paste(tree$tip.label[tip_idx], collapse = " ")
  })
)

# Add in the correct commands needed to reconstruct the ancestral states 
d_nodes <- mutate(
  d_nodes, 
  first_command = paste('AddTag', paste('T', nodes, sep = ''), label, sep = ' '), 
  second_command = paste('AddNode', nodes, paste('T', nodes, sep = ''), sep = ' ')
  )

# Output this as a vector
general_control_file_data <- select(d_nodes, first_command, second_command) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), values_to = 'command') %>%
  pull(command)

# Choose 1, 1 -> MultiState evolution model and Maximum Likelihood analysis method
general_control_file_data <- append("1", general_control_file_data)
general_control_file_data <- append("1", general_control_file_data)

# The rest of the logic is all feature specific so we loop over features

# Generate control file for each
for (id in features$id) {
  feature_control_file_data <- general_control_file_data
  log_file_name <- paste("../data/log", id, sep = "_")
  log_file_command <- paste("LogFile", log_file_name)
  feature_control_file_data <- append(feature_control_file_data, log_file_command)
  feature_control_file_data <- append(feature_control_file_data, "Run")
  control_file_name <- paste(
    paste("max_likelihood_control", id, sep = "_"),
    ".txt", 
    sep = ""
  )
  write.table(
  feature_control_file_data,
  here(paste("scripts/", control_file_name, sep = "")),
  quote=FALSE,
  col.names = FALSE,
  row.names = FALSE
  )

  ## 4. Run maximum likelihood analysis for each feature in BayesTraits
  
  # This needs to be done directly using BayesTraits in the command line because although rgriff23/btw is an R wrapper package for BayesTraits, it does not support versions beyond V3. It is used further below to parse the output log file however
  
  trait_file_name <- paste("../data/trait_", id, ".txt", sep = "")
    
  system(
    paste(
      BAYESTRAITS_PATH, 
      "../data/tree.nex",
      trait_file_name,
      "<",
      control_file_name
    )
  )
  
  ## 5. BayesTraits output reading for each feature
  
  # Read in output from BayesTraits
  output <- parse_log(paste(log_file_name, ".Log.txt", sep = ""))
  
  # Grab just the nodes and transform into a table with the probability of each state for each node
  d_output <- select(output$results, starts_with('X')) %>% 
     pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = 'var', values_to = 'prob') %>% 
     filter(!is.na(prob)) %>% 
     separate(col = var, c('node', 'p', 'state'), sep = '\\.') %>% 
     select(-p) %>% 
     mutate(node = gsub('X', '', node))
  
  # Transform into table with two columns for each state probability per node
  d_wide <- d_output %>%
    mutate(node = as.numeric(node)) %>%
    pivot_wider(
    names_from = state,
    values_from = prob,
    names_prefix = "prob_"
    )
  
  # Plot tree with trait values and state probabilities
  
  # Open PNG device to save plot
  plot_file_name <- paste("../plots/", id, ".png", sep = "")
  png(plot_file_name)

  tip_order   <- match(tree$tip.label, trait$language)
  tip_vals    <- as.character(trait$value[tip_order])
  tip_colors  <- ifelse(tip_vals == "1", "black", ifelse(tip_vals == "0", "white", "grey80"))
  
  col_ramp  <- colorRampPalette(c("lightblue", "darkblue"))
  node_cols <- col_ramp(100)[as.integer(cut(d_wide$prob_1,
                                            breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = 101),
                                            include.lowest = TRUE))]
  
  par(mar = c(1, 1, 2, 1))
  plot(tree, show.tip.label = TRUE, label.offset = 0.03,
       x.lim = c(0, max(ape::node.depth.edgelength(tree)) * 1.5))
  tiplabels(pch = 21, bg = tip_colors, col = "black", cex = 1.8, offset = 0.01)
  nodelabels(pch = 21, bg = node_cols, col = "black",
             node = d_wide$node, cex = 1.8)
  nodelabels(text = round(d_wide$prob_1, 2), node = d_wide$node,
             frame = "none", adj = c(-0.3, 0.5), cex = 0.75)
  legend("bottomleft", legend = c("state 0", "state 1", "missing"),
         pt.bg = c("white", "black", "grey80"), pch = 21, pt.cex = 1.5, bty = "n")
  legend("bottomright",
         legend = c("P(1) = 0", "P(1) = 0.5", "P(1) = 1"),
         pt.bg = col_ramp(3), pch = 21, pt.cex = 1.5, bty = "n",
         title = "Ancestral P(1)")
  
  # Close PNG device
  dev.off()
}
